# Transcrição: How_to_deliver_a_good_presentation

- **Fonte:** How_to_deliver_a_good_presentation
- **Duração:** 01:11
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 01:11]

Think about a song that you know the lyrics off by heart. How do you sing it? You sing it in the car expressively because you know all the lyrics you now can focus on the emotion. You can focus on all the feelings behind every single word in the song. Is that fair to say? Now think about a song you don't know the lyrics to. When you don't know the lyrics to a song, there's no way you have the same level of emotion and enthusiasm while you're singing it because you're trying to think of what the next words are. My friends, this is why rehearsal is so important because once you learn the words to what you're about to say, you now can focus on the emotions of what you're saying. So the way to speak that doesn't strip away all the emotion and all the wonderful qualities in your voice, the way to do that is by proper rehearsal and by memorising and learning the words that you're going to be speaking. Now when I say memorising, it's memorising with the freedom for a little bit of improv. You don't have to say word for word, but you need to know exactly what you want to say so that you can give yourself the creative freedom and the comfort to be able to move away from that if you want to. People often say, and I'll repeat here again, the more you rehearse, people go, does that make you more robotic? No, no, no, no. The more you rehearse, the more natural you get.
